task_completion.description: Enable cash rounding and set the interval to 0.05.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the NCH Copper POS settings/options menu, the cash rounding feature is checked/enabled and the rounding interval or denomination is set to 0.05.

task_completion.description: Set the rounding rule to 'Round to the nearest value'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the NCH Copper POS settings/options menu, the rounding rule dropdown or selection is set to 'Round to the nearest value' (or 'Nearest').

task_completion.description: Add 'Washer Test' item priced at $0.03 to the transaction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main POS transaction screen shows an item named 'Washer Test' with a price of $0.03 added to the current sale.

task_completion.description: Add 'Bolt Test' item priced at $1.00 to the transaction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main POS transaction screen shows an item named 'Bolt Test' with a price of $1.00 added to the current sale.

task_completion.description: Process the transaction as a cash sale.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The payment screen or transaction log shows that 'Cash' was selected as the payment method for the transaction.

task_completion.description: Complete the transaction and display the final receipt showing the rounded total of $1.05.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A final receipt or transaction summary is visible on the screen. It must show the pre-round total of $1.03, a rounding adjustment (+$0.02), and a final total of $1.05.

integrity.description: The agent used the NCH Copper POS graphical user interface to configure settings and process the sale.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the actual NCH Copper POS menus, settings dialogs, and transaction screens.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or direct database edits to bypass the POS configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open showing SQLite commands or script executions modifying the POS database directly.

integrity.description: The final receipt is genuinely generated by the POS software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The receipt or transaction summary is displayed within the NCH Copper POS interface or its native receipt viewer, not typed out in Notepad or a generic text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required rounding interval is 0.05 and the rule is 'Round to the nearest value' (or 'Nearest'). The test items 'Washer Test' ($0.03) and 'Bolt Test' ($1.00) create a pre-round total of $1.03. Using standard symmetric rounding to the nearest 0.05 interval, the final total must be exactly $1.05 (a +$0.02 adjustment). Rounding typically only applies to Cash transactions.
